generatedSentence: Earlier, in the brushy junkyard, their freedom felt kaput, a bankrupt world everywhere. The milky monsoon, like watercolour, washed over organic growth; the heroic navigator's blackeye throbbed, his religion and all voices gone. With a rudimentary hayfork and clunky jackets, the duo, known for their chess tactics, injected a vague hope. They visited a maidservant, having earlier doubted the receiver's gossip, knowing poisonous monsters now abound, jeopardizing all privacy. The pandemonium began, a gladiator’s duel for justice, as bayonets and a crushing virus spread from the gulag. Rational masters owning a hollow vision decided the trial, while the world watched the whale-like castle loom, fury rising. Through the neon tollway, the keeper, energizing the action, delivered the verdict. Recycled jugs of alcohol and pineapple mixtures, once banned, now symbolized new dignity; the singing waiter shared an apple-pasta, and their kissing felt tangible, not martyry.
panelSentences: Earlier, in the brushy junkyard, their freedom felt kaput, a bankrupt world everywhere. The milky monsoon, like watercolour, washed over organic growth; the heroic navigator's blackeye throbbed, his religion and all voices gone.

With a rudimentary hayfork and clunky jackets, the duo, known for their chess tactics, injected a vague hope. They visited a maidservant, having earlier doubted the receiver's gossip, knowing poisonous monsters now abound, jeopardizing all privacy.

The pandemonium began, a gladiator’s duel for justice, as bayonets and a crushing virus spread from the gulag. Rational masters owning a hollow vision decided the trial, while the world watched the whale-like castle loom, fury rising.

Through the neon tollway, the keeper, energizing the action, delivered the verdict. Recycled jugs of alcohol and pineapple mixtures, once banned, now symbolized new dignity; the singing waiter shared an apple-pasta, and their kissing felt tangible, not martyry.

The pandemonium began, a gladiator’s duel for justice, as bayonets and a crushing virus spread from the gulag. Rational masters owning a hollow vision decided the trial, while the world watched the whale-like castle loom, fury rising.

Through the neon tollway, the keeper, energizing the action, delivered the verdict. Recycled jugs of alcohol and pineapple mixtures, once banned, now symbolized new dignity; the singing waiter shared an apple-pasta, and their kissing felt tangible, not martyry.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a cybernetic navigator wearing a patched-up, glowing blue flight jacket and a silver visor that covers a blackeye. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, seen from a low angle, standing in a vast, rain-swept junkyard under a milky monsoon sky, looking at a broken organic data terminal. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist and their duo partner, seen from a medium shot, exchanging a rudimentary hayfork with a worried maidservant in a dimly lit, tech-baroque interior. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, in a dynamic wide shot, engaged in a desperate gladiator-style duel with shadowy figures holding futuristic bayonets, a colossal, whale-like castle looming in the background. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist and their partner, seen from a close-up, sharing a peaceful, tangible kiss, a neon tollway glowing softly in the background, a repurposed jug of pineapple drink nearby.

definitions.definition: Any of a number of winds associated with regions where most rain falls during a particular season.

The rains themselves.
Entire meteorological systems with such characteristics.

(color science) Of the black in an image, appearing as dark gray rather than black.
(of a drink) Containing (an especially large amount of) milk.

Cowardly.
Immature, childish.
Producing milk, lactating.
The state of being free, of not being imprisoned or enslaved.

definitions.definition: The lack of a specific constraint, or of constraints in general; a state of being free, unconstrained.

definitions.definition: Frankness; openness; unreservedness.

Of a military unit or formation, or its elements, belonging to a permanent organization (in contrast to being temporarily attached).
Instrumental; acting as instruments of nature or of art to a certain destined function or end.
(of search results) Generated according to the ranking algorithms of a search engine, as opposed to paid placement by advertisers.

Recently born.

definitions.definition: Having a favourable position, such as a position of influence or expected gain, in relation to another person.

definitions.definition: At or towards the interior of a defined space, such as a building or room.
definitions.example: He ran to the edge of the swimming pool and dived in.

definitions.definition: Towards the speaker or other reference point.
definitions.example: For six hours the tide flows in, then for another six hours it flows out.

definitions.definition: So as to be enclosed or surrounded by something.
definitions.example: Bring the water to the boil and drop the vegetables in.

definitions.definition: Still eligible to play, e.g. able to bat in cricket and baseball.
definitions.example: He went for the wild toss but wasn't able to stay in.

definitions.definition: After the beginning of something.
definitions.example: The show still didn't become interesting 20 minutes in.

definitions.definition: (in combination, after a verb) Denotes a gathering of people assembled for the stated activity, sometimes, though not always, suggesting a protest.
Used to indicate location, inclusion, or position within spatial, temporal or other limits.
Into.
definitions.example: Less water gets in your boots this way.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate limit, qualification, condition, or circumstance.
definitions.example: In replacing the faucet washers, he felt he was making his contribution to the environment.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate means, medium, format, genre, or instrumentality.
Something that surrounds; encompassing material, substance or shape.
The atmosphere; the surrounding air or sky; atmospheric components collectively such as air, clouds, water vapour, hail, etc.
A type of modern music that creates a relaxing and peaceful atmosphere.
Encompassing on all sides; surrounding; encircling; enveloping.
definitions.example: A cup of tea eventually cools to the ambient temperature.

definitions.definition: Evoking or creating an atmosphere: atmospheric.
Relating to, or suitable for, storage at room temperature.

definitions.definition: Containing objects or describing a setting that one is interested in.
A tool used for moving hay; a pitchfork.
A person who works undercover.
To provide too little coverage.
Performed or happening in secret.
Employed or engaged in spying or secret investigation.
Containing sufficient poison to be dangerous to touch or ingest.
Negative, harmful.
(in ancient Rome) A person (professional or slave) who entertained the public by engaging in mortal combat with another, or with a wild animal.
(by extension) A disputant in a public controversy or debate.
A professional boxer.
To fight as entertainment for others.
To compete in a public contest.
To debate or argue.
To act aggressively toward others.
Any of various game birds of the subfamily Tetraoninae which inhabit temperate and subarctic regions of the northern hemisphere.
To seek or shoot grouse.
A person who or thing that receives or is intended to receive something. More formal, usually referring to one who receives such things as an award or medal.
A toll road or toll highway, where a fee is charged in order to travel on it; a turnpike.
One who keeps something.

definitions.definition: A person or thing worth keeping.
A person charged with guarding or caring for, storing, or maintaining something; a custodian, a guard; sometimes a gamekeeper.
The player charged with guarding a goal or wicket. Short form of goalkeeper, wicketkeeper.
A part of a mechanism that catches or retains another part, for example the part of a door lock that fits in the frame and receives the bolt.
An offensive play in which the quarterback runs toward the goal with the ball after it is snapped.
One who remains or keeps in a place or position.
A fruit or vegetable that keeps for some time without spoiling.
A terrifying and dangerous creature.
A bizarre or whimsical creature.

definitions.definition: A cruel or antisocial person, especially a criminal.
definitions.example: Get away from those children, you meatheaded monster!

definitions.definition: A horribly deformed person.
A badly behaved child, a brat.
definitions.example: Sit still, you little monster!

definitions.definition: Something unusually large.
definitions.example: Have you seen those powerlifters on TV? They're monsters.

definitions.definition: A prodigy; someone very talented in a specific domain.

definitions.definition: A non-player character that player(s) fight against in role-playing games.
To make into a monster; to categorise as a monster; to demonise.
To behave as a monster to; to terrorise.
To harass.
(with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.

definitions.definition: The Universe.
(with "the") The Earth.

definitions.definition: A planet, especially one which is inhabited or inhabitable.

definitions.definition: A very large extent of country.
(speculation) A realm, such as planet, containing one or multiple societies of beings, especially intelligent ones.

definitions.definition: An individual or group perspective or social setting.

definitions.definition: The part of an operating system distributed with the kernel, consisting of the shell and other programs.
A subdivision of a game, consisting of a series of stages or levels that usually share a similar environment or theme.

definitions.definition: The twenty-second trump or major arcana card of the tarot.
A great amount.

definitions.definition: Age, era

Something, or someone, that is very large.

A small valley between mountains.

To invigorate; to make energetic.

definitions.definition: To use strength in action; to act or operate with force or vigor; to act in producing an effect.
A flexible container made of cloth, paper, plastic, etc.
A handbag
A suitcase.
A schoolbag, especially a backpack.
One’s preference.

definitions.definition: An ugly woman.
The cloth-covered pillow used for first, second, and third base.

definitions.definition: (preceded by "the") A breathalyzer, so named because it formerly had a plastic bag over the end to measure a set amount of breath.
A collection of objects, disregarding order, but (unlike a set) in which elements may be repeated.
definitions.example: If one has a bag of three apples and the letter 'a' is taken to denote 'apple', then such bag could be represented symbolically as {a,a,a}. Note that in an ordinary context, when talking about a bag of apples, one does not care about identifying the individual apples, although one might be interested in distinguishing apples by species, for example, letting 'r' denote 'red apple' and 'g' denote 'green apple', then a bag of three red apples and two green apples could be denoted as {r,r,r,g,g}.

definitions.definition: A sac in animal bodies, containing some fluid or other substance.

definitions.definition: A pouch tied behind a man's head to hold the back-hair of a wig; a bag wig.

A scrotum.
A unit of measure of cement equal to 94 pounds.

£1000, a grand.
A large number or amount.
To put into a bag.
To catch or kill, especially when fishing or hunting.

To become pregnant.

To curse; to execrate.

definitions.definition: To curse; to utter curses or maledictions.
Forbidden; not allowed.
A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An instance of castling.
A rook; a chess piece shaped like a castle tower.
A defense structure in shogi formed by defensive pieces surrounding the king.

Any strong, imposing, and stately mansion.

The wicket.

To protect or separate in a similar way.
To make into a castle: to build in the form of a castle or add (real or imitation) battlements to an existing building.

(usually intransitive) To move the king 2 squares right or left and, in the same turn, the nearest rook to the far side of the king. The move now has special rules: the king cannot be in, go through, or end in check; the squares between the king and rook must be vacant; and neither piece may have been moved before castling.
(usually intransitive) To create a similar defensive position in Japanese chess through several moves.
To bowl a batsman with a full-length ball or yorker such that the stumps are knocked over.
A utensil; tool; a weapon; (usually in compound) an article in general.

A mapping from a pairing of mathematical objects to one of them, respecting their individual structures. The pairing is typically a Cartesian product or a tensor product. The object that is not part of the output is said to act on the other object. A pointed instrument of the dagger kind fitted on the muzzle of a musket or rifle, so as to give the soldier increased means of offence and defence. Originally, the bayonet was made with a handle, which needed to be fitted into the bore of the musket after the soldier had fired.

[Gulag: Soviet Prison Camps and Their Legacy; By David Hosford, Pamela Kachurin and Thomas Lamont. National Resource Center for Russian, East European and Central Asian Studies, Harvard University]

definitions.definition: To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
That kisses.
Just touching.
Ungainly; awkward; inelegant; cumbersome.
Being or making a clunk sound.
To ransack.
In disrepair or disorder; poorly maintained; lacking upkeep, usually of buildings or vehicles.
definitions.example: He entered the ramshackle bus, and was driven a long distance through very sandy streets to the hotel on the St. Lawrence.
